title: Catalan Catastrophes: How Alex Baker Won a Trophy by Eight While Setting Fire to the Scorecard
title_candidates: Catalan Catastrophes: How Alex Baker Won a Trophy by Eight While Setting Fire to the Scorecard
The Eleven, the Ten, and the Trophy: Alex Baker's Curiously Dominant TEG 18
Camiral, Mon Amour: Sun, Stadium and Sextuples
theme: A wire-to-wire Trophy procession by a champion who also authored some of the most spectacular blow-ups of the week — Alex Baker scoring 46-41-44-38 with an 11, a 10 and an 8 thrown in for entertainment — while Gregg Williams quietly assembled a Green Jacket rout and Jon Baker reluctantly accepted the Spoon.
opening_hook: Open on Alex Baker walking off the 18th green at the Tour course in R2 having just signed for an eleven — and still leading the Trophy by 11 points. Then rewind to how a champion can post a sextuple and barely notice.
foreshadow: Alex Baker's lead in R1 is already 12 — the rest of the week is housekeeping
The Stadium course is lurking on the back half of the week with teeth bared
Gregg Williams is the Gross runner-up after R1 despite a quadruple at the 7th — a hint he's the Jacket man-in-waiting
Jon Baker is bottom of the Trophy by nightfall on day one

competitions.name: Trophy (Stableford)
competitions.how: Baker seized outright lead at R1 H5 after a brief David Mullin cameo at H4, and never looked behind him again: leads of 12, 11, 12, 8 across the four rounds. The 46-point opener at the Tour was the knockout punch; he then defended a double-figure cushion through three rounds despite three sextuple/quintuple-plus catastrophes. Runner-up John Patterson never got within touching distance. Final margin: 8 points (169 plays 161).

competitions.name: Green Jacket (Gross)
competitions.how: Jon Baker led the Gross after R1 (+2) and R2, but Gregg Williams — who'd been third after a R1 that included a quadruple at the 7th and another at the 17th — took outright lead at the 2nd hole of R3 on the Stadium course and never gave it back. Stadium rounds of 13 and 12 over par turned a two-shot deficit into a 14-shot rout. Runner-up: David Mullin.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Bottom after R1, briefly relieved of the burden by David Mullin in R2, but reclaimed it definitively at the 2nd hole of R3 (a triple bogey on the Stadium opener) and never escaped again. Rounds of 30-39-28-29 totalled 126 points — well clear of the field at the wrong end. A quintuple at the Stadium 4th in R3 was the centrepiece.

headline_candidates: 46 and Gone: Baker Lays Down the Law at the Tour
Sextuples at Dawn: Mullin's Eleven Sets the Tone
rounds.chosen_headline: 46 and Gone: Baker Lays Down the Law at the Tour
rounds.angle: Alex Baker's 46-point opener — built on a 17-point burst from holes 8-12 — opens up a 12-point Trophy lead that effectively decides the tournament on day one. Meanwhile John Patterson bleeds 22 shots in eight holes, David Mullin runs up an 11 at the 15th, and Jon Baker quietly assumes the Spoon position by sundown.

Alex Baker signs for an 11 at the Tour's 18th and still extends his Trophy lead in spirit if not arithmetic. Jon Baker plays the best closing five of the week (16 points, 14-18) but the damage has been done; he drops to the Spoon spot mid-round anyway. Gregg Williams quietly stacks pars on the back nine.

headline_candidates: Stadium Day: Williams Pounces, Jon Baker Slips
Two-Hole Coronation: Gregg Takes the Jacket at the 2nd
Baker Bleeds Twelve and Still Leads by Twelve
rounds.chosen_headline: Stadium Day: Williams Pounces, Jon Baker Slips
rounds.angle: The Stadium course rearranges the secondary battles. Gregg Williams takes outright Gross lead at the 2nd hole and is gone; on the very same hole Jon Baker drops into the Spoon position he won't escape. Alex Baker bleeds 12 shots from 6-10 and answers with a 21-point binge from 11-16. The Trophy lead: still 12.

headline_candidates: One More Ten for the Road
rounds.angle: The processional final round. Williams closes out the Jacket at +12 for the day, winning by 14. Alex Baker treats himself to a farewell 10 at the 14th and an 8 at the 17th, signs for 38 points anyway, and wins the Trophy by 8. Jon Baker's birdie at the 13th breaks an eleven-hole bogey-or-worse streak; the Spoon is his.

players.arc: Wire-to-wire Trophy champion who turned the leaderboard into a procession on day one and then spent the rest of the week alternating between Stableford masterclasses and gross-card pyrotechnics — an 11, a 10, an 8, and two further quadruples — without ever losing the lead.
Started third in the Gross with a R1 that included quadruples at the 7th and 17th, then quietly went 14-13-12 over par across rounds 2-4 to win the Green Jacket by fourteen.
Led the Gross after R1 and R2 — a Green Jacket contender on Tuesday night — before the Stadium course found him out on Monday and dropped him into a Spoon he never escaped.
Trophy runner-up by the simple expedient of not being Alex Baker; bookended a calamitous 22-shot bleed in R1 with smart back-nine rallies and an excellent R2 stretch.
An eleven at the 15th in R1, three further quadruples across the Tour course, and yet finished Gross runner-up — handicapping in action.

venue_notes: TEG's 4th visit to Catalonia, Spain — all four rounds at Camiral Golf & Wellness (PGA Catalunya). Frame R1-R2 as the Tour course (the 'forgiving sibling', 5th & 6th TEG rounds there) and R3-R4 as the Stadium (Spain's premier course, European-Tour designed, 6th & 7th TEG rounds there). Use the Tour/Stadium step-up as the natural pivot of the week: secondary races (Jacket, Spoon) all turn on the Stadium opening holes.
